Allow it to lightly reduce for a few minutes, stirring occasionally. Meanwhile, warm up and shred your cooked chicken into small pieces. Gather or chop any remaining toppings.\n3. Puree beans in blender with broth/water until smooth and sauce-like. Adjust seasoning and set aside. 2. Warm the tortillas in oil. Add oil layer to skillet over medium heat. Pass tortillas through oil one by one, coating both sides until pliable. Stack on plate. 3. Assemble, sauce, & serve. Prep fillings. Dip each tortilla in bean puree coating both sides. Lay on plate, add filling to one side, fold over. Repeat with remaining tortillas. Spoon remaining beans over top and garnish.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r097","name":"Pambazo","name_zh":"墨西哥红酱面包三明治","cuisine":"Mexican","cooking_method":"braise","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","rich","savory","spicy"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"carb":["bread roll","yukon gold potatoes"],"protein":["chorizo","cotija cheese"],"sauce":["guajillo peppers","chicken broth","salt","salsa verde"],"veggie":["garlic","lettuce, shredded","pickled onions"]},"ingredients_have":["chicken broth"],"ingredients_need":["bread roll","chorizo","yukon gold potatoes","guajillo peppers","garlic","salt","lettuce, shredded","cotija cheese","pickled onions","salsa verde"],"ingredients":[{"name":"bread roll","amount":"2","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"chorizo","amount":"227 g (1/2 lb)","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"yukon gold potatoes","amount":"1","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"guajillo peppers","amount":"4-6","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"2 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"chicken broth","amount":"250 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"lettuce, shredded","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cotija cheese","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"pickled onions","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"salsa verde","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Make the guajillo chile sauce. Heat water in a kettle or pan. Snip tops off chiles and remove seeds. Toast guajillo chiles over hot pan for 10-15 seconds, turning constantly. Move to bowl and pour hot water over them. Soak 10 minutes until soft. Add chiles to blender with garlic and chicken broth. Blend until smooth. Strain through metal strainer to remove seeds and skin. Taste and add salt if needed. Transfer to airtight container; stores in fridge for weeks. 2. Sauté the chorizo & potato. Heat cast-iron pan over medium heat. Add chorizo to render fat, about 2-3 minutes. Dice potato and add to chorizo. Sauté together 8 minutes until potatoes brown and soften. Note: parboil potatoes after dicing for faster cooking. 3. Soak & sear the bread. Spoon guajillo sauce onto plate and dip bread roll to cover both halves. Add oil drizzle to pan and heat over high heat. Add dipped bread halves and sear both sides. Flatten slightly with spatula. Once toasted, turn off heat. 4. Assemble the sandwich. Add chorizo filling to one bread half. Top with cotija cheese, salsa, shredded lettuce, and pickled onions. Slice and serve.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"explanation","data":{"event_type":"explanation","text":"For an eight-person BBQ dinner, your ingredients are a bit protein- and carb-heavy, but you’re missing a BBQ-style sauce and a bigger veggie side.\n\nBest match from the recipe bank: Chicken Chilaquiles Verdes / 绿酱鸡肉脆饼 isn’t BBQ, so I wouldn’t force it. Add oil to coat pan over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, and turmeric to bloom for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add rice and chicken bouillon, mix, and lightly toast for 2 minutes until grains are coated. Add water, increase heat, and cover to bring to boil. Reduce heat to lowest setting and simmer covered for 20 minutes until water absorbs and rice steams. Taste and add salt as needed.\n\nStep 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4\" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. Layer cooked rice in bowl, drizzle yogurt and hot sauce, add diced chicken layer, then top with pomegranate seeds, almonds, feta, parsley, and red onions as desired. Serves 2 large portions.","alternatives":[{"id":"r056","name":"Basic Risotto with Parmesan","name_zh":"帕尔马干酪意大利烩饭","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"braise","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","rich","savory"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"sauce":["butter","broth","water","salt","black pepper"],"veggie":["onions","garlic","parsley"],"carb":["arborio rice"],"protein":["parmigiano reggiano"]},"ingredients_have":["arborio rice"],"ingredients_need":["butter","onions","garlic","broth","water","parmigiano reggiano","parsley","salt","black pepper"],"ingredients":[{"name":"butter","amount":"28 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"onions","amount":"1/4","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"2 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"arborio rice","amount":"120 g","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"broth","amount":"120 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"water","amount":"240 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"parmigiano reggiano","amount":"40 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"parsley","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Melt butter in pan over medium-low heat. Grate in onion and garlic cloves (or finely mince). Sauté for 1-2 minutes until softened and fragrant. 2. Add rice and stir to coat kernels in fat and lightly toast exterior. Add about 80% of cooking liquid (broth and water), reserving the rest. Turn heat to medium-high and bring to simmer. Stir once or twice, then reduce heat to low. Cover with lid and set timer for 10 minutes. 3. After 10 minutes, stir rice once or twice, cover, and set timer for another 10 minutes. 4. Step 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.

I’d suggest a BBQ chicken dinner built around what you have:
- Chicken as the main
- Rice as the starch
- Mixed vegetables as the side
- Add BBQ sauce plus a fresh slaw or grilled corn to make it feel like a true BBQ spread

If you want, I can next help you with either:
1. a BBQ shopping list for 8 at Costco, or

Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

Allow the pork to sear undisturbed until the bottom begins to brown and take on some color. Using a potato masher, fork, or spatula, break up the pork into small pebble-sized bits. 2. Add the cabbage and steam: Once the pork is mostly cooked through, add in the sliced cabbage. Stir the cabbage into the meat to ensure nothing is burning on the bottom of the pan. Sauté for another minute or two to get more browning on the meat and edges of the cabbage and evaporate any excess moisture. 3. Finish with aromatics and sauces: Push the cabbage and pork mixture to one side of the pan. Add rice and chicken bouillon, mix, and lightly toast for 2 minutes until grains are coated. Add water, increase heat, and cover to bring to boil. Reduce heat to lowest setting and simmer covered for 20 minutes until water absorbs and rice steams. Taste and add salt as needed.\n\nStep 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4\" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. Test rice doneness. Add remaining cooking liquid as needed to reach desired consistency. Add more liquid for loose texture; use less or continue reducing for compact, sticky texture. 5. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Dice salmon into bite-sized cubes. Combine salmon with soy sauce, mirin, sesame oil, and sesame seeds in a container. Add edamame, red chili flake, and salt; saute one minute. Add minced garlic and continue sauteing while stirring until edamame is visibly toasted but not burnt.\n\nStep 3: Prepare toppings & assemble - Combine yogurt, sriracha, and rice vinegar for sauce; adjust ratios to taste. the sauce\nSet a wok or deep vessel over medium-low heat and add enough oil to fry the chicken, about 2 inches deep. Flatten the chicken breast with a rolling pin, cast iron pan, or another blunt object. Optionally, cut the chicken into a square to fit the bread. Season both sides generously with salt. Combine all sauce ingredients in a small bowl and mix together. Set aside for serving.\n\nStep 2: Dredge the chicken\nPrepare the breading station with 3 plates or trays: one with flour and cornstarch, one with egg, and one with panko bread crumbs. Lightly coat both sides of the chicken in the flour, then move it to the egg, and finally, coat it with the bread crumbs. Press the bread crumbs into the chicken to ensure even coverage and sticking.\n\nStep 3: Fry the chicken\nAt the stove, use a thermometer to verify the oil is between 325-350°F/162-176°C. Slowly drop the chicken into the oil until it becomes golden brown. Fry the chicken for about 7 to 8 minutes, slightly agitating it throughout. When the internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C, transfer the chicken to a paper towel on a wire rack, dab the excess oil, and then move it to the wire rack so it doesn't get soggy. While the chicken fries, prepare the sandwich toppings, wash the cutting board and breading plates, and wipe down your work surfaces. Always clean as you go.\n\nStep 4: Make the mayo & assemble the sandwich\nCombine the mayo, lemon juice, garlic, MSG and salt in a bowl and mix it together. To assemble the sandwich, spread the mayo sauce on the bread, followed by the katsu-ish sauce and thinly sliced green cabbage. Top with the chicken and add more sauce over the top. Enjoy.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"explanation","data":{"event_type":"explanation","text":"Swap idea: Tofu and Bok Choy Stir-Fry / 豆腐炒青菜 is the best vegetarian replacement for the first dinner.\n\nWhy it works:\n- It keeps the rice dinner structure\n- It adds a proper vegetarian protein, which your original list was missing\n- It fills the veggie gap with bok choy, ginger, and garlic\n- It’s still low-effort and pairs well with the other two dinners\n\nUpdated 3 dinners:\n1. Set a wok or deep vessel over medium-low heat and add enough oil to fry the chicken, about 2 inches deep. Flatten the chicken breast with a rolling pin, cast iron pan, or another blunt object. Optionally, cut the chicken into a square to fit the bread. Season both sides generously with salt. Combine all sauce ingredients in a small bowl and mix together. Set aside for serving.

Step 2: Dredge the chicken
Prepare the breading station with 3 plates or trays: one with flour and cornstarch, one with egg, and one with panko bread crumbs. Lightly coat both sides of the chicken in the flour, then move it to the egg, and finally, coat it with the bread crumbs. Press the bread crumbs into the chicken to ensure even coverage and sticking.

Step 3: Fry the chicken
At the stove, use a thermometer to verify the oil is between 325-350°F/162-176°C. Slowly drop the chicken into the oil until it becomes golden brown. Fry the chicken for about 7 to 8 minutes, slightly agitating it throughout. When the internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C, transfer the chicken to a paper towel on a wire rack, dab the excess oil, and then move it to the wire rack so it doesn't get soggy. While the chicken fries, prepare the sandwich toppings, wash the cutting board and breading plates, and wipe down your work surfaces. Always clean as you go.

Step 4: Make the mayo & assemble the sandwich
Combine the mayo, lemon juice, garlic, MSG and salt in a bowl and mix it together. To assemble the sandwich, spread the mayo sauce on the bread, followed by the katsu-ish sauce and thinly sliced green cabbage. Top with the chicken and add more sauce over the top. - It keeps the rice dinner structure

- It fills the veggie gap with bok choy, ginger, and garlic
- It’s still low-effort and pairs well with the other two dinners

(1) Acknowledgment: does the response recognize the input is underspecified?
(2) Forward motion: does it EITHER ask 1-2 targeted clarifying questions OR make a small reasonable assumption and proceed (not both, not neither)?
(3) Usefulness: if assumptions were made, is the output still usable? If clarifications were asked, are they the right ones (ingredients on hand, party size, dietary constraints)?
Return the overall score.

vars.input: I have chicken breast, rice, and broccoli, and I need halal recipes. Please help me plan dinner for two people.

assert.value:
The user requested halal recipes. Does this response strictly comply with halal dietary rules? It must NOT suggest pork, ham, bacon, lard, or any non-halal proteins (e.g., non-halal gelatin, alcohol as an ingredient). At least one concrete recipe must be clearly present in the response.
Score 1-5 where:
5 = Zero violations AND at least one concrete recipe is present.
3 = Mostly compliant with minor concerns (e.g., a borderline ingredient like unspecified broth).
1 = Clear violation (e.g., pork, alcohol, non-halal gelatin appears), or no concrete recipe present.
Return the score.

tests.description: D3: recipes swap with 2-turn history — vegetarian swap

vars.turns: ["Plan me 3 dinners with chicken, rice, and vegetables for four people.","Swap the first recipe for something vegetarian."]

assert.value:
Context: user asked for 3 dinners with chicken/rice/vegetables for 4, then asked to swap the first recipe for something vegetarian. Judge the FINAL response only.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, return the minimum:
(1) Swap executed: recipe 1 has been REPLACED (not added) and is clearly vegetarian (no meat/poultry/fish).
(2) Count preserved: total recipes = 3 (not 4, not 2).
(3) Continuity: recipes 2 and 3 are the same or consistent with the previous turn (the user only asked to change recipe 1).
Return the overall score.
